messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "intelligent-subset-scope-mismatch",
 "title": "The venture sells intelligent PDUs; the TAM counts all rack PDUs — the category boundary mismatch is inside the model, not at its edge",
 "narrative": "Every boundary discussion has focused on excluded substitutes (busway, shelves). The unexamined boundary is in the other direction: the product is intelligent rack PDUs, but tamBase is explicitly scoped as the 'Central Europe rack-PDU market, all segments/buyers' — including basic and metered-only units the venture does not sell. IndexBox itself maintains a separate EU 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' report distinct from its 'Rack PDUs' report, i.e., the industry's own taxonomy treats these as different products. No retrieved source states the intelligent share of European rack-PDU revenue, and the 10–12% intelligent growth figure the model cites traces to a far broader power-and-cable-management report. The ±40% band prices measurement error on the whole category; it says nothing about what fraction of that category the venture can even quote into. Both errors stack.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "tamBase"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "The product is intelligent rack PDUs while tamBase covers all rack PDUs; IndexBox sells 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' and 'Rack PDUs' as separate EU reports, confirming the vendor taxonomy splits the subset the venture sells from the category the model sized",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Product",
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "digest:tam-base#c14"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The intelligent subset's cited 10–12% growth comes from the €8.5–9.5B EU Power-and-Cable-Management report — a category far broader than rack PDUs — and the directly-scoped rack-panel figure grows only 5–8%; no source quantifies the intelligent share of EU rack-PDU revenue, so the subset fraction is unknown and plausibly well below 1.0",
   "refs": [
    "digest:tam-base#c4",
    "digest:tam-base#c5"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The €300M base overstates the venture's category-true TAM by whatever revenue share basic/metered-only PDUs hold in CE; the sensitivity band (240–360) models measurement error of the full category, orthogonal to and stackable with the subset mismatch — and the one report that would resolve it sits behind a $4,000 paywall",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:tamBase",
    "digest:tam-base#c12"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "'Rack PDU' reads as one category and intelligent PDUs are its premium tier, so value share is assumed to approximate category revenue; the wide ±40% band creates the comfortable impression that any downside is already priced in. The band and the subset mismatch are independent errors, and the founder's risk list treats the band as covering both.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs headline (or the Omdia rack-PDU tracker's segmentation) showing intelligent/managed units at ≥80% of EU rack-PDU revenue, confirming the subset ≈ category in value terms.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "Data-center buyers skew heavily toward metered/switched units and intelligent SKUs carry a large price premium, which pulls value share up — but basic PDUs still dominate unit volumes in enterprise and retail-colo racks, and no source pins the revenue split; genuine coin-flip territory on whether the mismatch exceeds ~25% of the base",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "tamBase",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.65,
   "note": "Intelligent (monitored/switched) PDUs assumed to hold ~65% of CE rack-PDU revenue; the remainder is basic/metered volume outside the venture's product boundary"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Published intelligent-vs-total rack-PDU revenue split for EU or Germany",
   "where": "IndexBox EU/Germany Intelligent Rack PDUs report headlines; Omdia Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis abstract updates",
   "threshold": "Intelligent share reported below 70% of category revenue",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "SKU mix stocked and quoted by CE distributors (basic vs metered vs switched/intelligent)",
   "where": "CE electrical/IT distributor price lists and catalogs (e.g., Rexel, Also/Ingram DC-infrastructure lines)",
   "threshold": "Basic+metered-only SKUs constituting the majority of listed rack-PDU line items",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission the IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs report (and Germany country cut) to pin the subset share directly",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "tamBase",
   "note": "~$4–8K resolves both the subset fraction and narrows the category band — the ledger's own promotion path, extended to the correct sub-report"
  },
  {
   "action": "Add a low-cost metered-only SKU tier to the launch portfolio so quotes can cover the full category while the intelligent tier carries margin",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Converts the boundary mismatch from foreclosed demand into an entry wedge"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "boundary-substitution",
 "riskId": "risk.boundary-substitution.intelligent-subset-scope-mismatch"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

IndexBox itself maintains a separate EU 'Intelligent Rack PDUs' report distinct from its 'Rack PDUs' report, i.e., the industry's own taxonomy treats these as different products. No retrieved source states the intelligent share of European rack-PDU revenue, and the 10–12% intelligent growth figure the model cites traces to a far broader power-and-cable-management report. the remainder is basic/metered volume outside the venture's product boundary"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Published intelligent-vs-total rack-PDU revenue split for EU or Germany","where":"IndexBox EU/Germany Intelligent Rack PDUs report headlines; Omdia Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis abstract updates","threshold":"Intelligent share reported below 70% of category revenue","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"SKU mix stocked and quoted by CE distributors (basic vs metered vs switched/intelligent)","where":"CE electrical/IT distributor price lists and catalogs (e.g., Rexel, Also/Ingram DC-infrastructure lines)","threshold":"Basic+metered-only SKUs constituting the majority of listed rack-PDU line items","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission the IndexBox EU Intelligent Rack PDUs report (and Germany country cut) to pin the subset share directly","type":"information","voiNodeId":"tamBase","note":"~$4–8K resolves both the subset fraction and narrows the category band — the ledger's own promotion path, extended to the correct sub-report"},{"action":"Add a low-cost metered-only SKU tier to the launch portfolio so quotes can cover the full category while the intelligent tier carries margin","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Converts the boundary mismatch from foreclosed demand into an entry wedge"}]}
